reviews.title: Great base in Chicago
reviews.text: We booked The Emily Hotel for a short Chicago stay. We used Morgan station more than expected, and it helped with timing. Housekeeping did a strong job; towels, counters and floors were consistently clean. The room felt thoughtfully designed, with enough space around the bed and desk. For a city stay, the hotel worked very well.

reviews.title: Comfortable and convenient
reviews.text: I chose The Emily Hotel because I wanted to be in West Loop. We could reach most plans without thinking too hard about transportation. Staff were polite, but requests for towels and room help took repeated calls. I noticed clean linens and a tidy bathroom throughout the stay. The internet connection was steady enough for work, maps and streaming.

reviews.title: Nice hotel, a few issues
reviews.text: We booked The Emily Hotel for a short Chicago stay. We used Morgan station more than expected, and it helped with timing. Wi-Fi stayed reliable during video calls and evening browsing. Housekeeping did a strong job; towels, counters and floors were consistently clean. Breakfast was available, but it did not feel worth planning around.

reviews.title: Nice hotel, a few issues
reviews.text: I chose The Emily Hotel because I wanted to be in West Loop. Staff were polite, but requests for towels and room help took repeated calls. The bed was comfortable, and the room layout worked well for unpacking and relaxing. Breakfast was limited for the price, so we went elsewhere the next morning. I would stay again, but only if the rate made sense.

reviews.title: Convenient but not perfect
reviews.text: We booked The Emily Hotel for a short Chicago stay. The hotel details, including rooftop lounge, terrace, fitness center and two restaurants, gave the stay a polished feel. The bed was comfortable, and the room layout worked well for unpacking and relaxing. Breakfast was limited for the price, so we went elsewhere the next morning. I liked the stay overall, with a couple of practical caveats.

reviews.title: Comfortable and convenient
reviews.text: We booked The Emily Hotel for a short Chicago stay. The location near West Loop restaurants, United Center, Millennium Park and Michigan Avenue shaped most of our plans. Value was the main drawback, especially for a short stay. The seating and bed were comfortable enough for a two-night stay. The bathroom and bedding felt crisp, which made the stay easier to trust.

reviews.title: Good stay with trade-offs
reviews.text: I stayed at The Emily Hotel during a local staycation. The location near West Loop restaurants, United Center, Millennium Park and Michigan Avenue shaped most of our plans. Wi-Fi connected quickly and did not drop during my stay. The room felt thoughtfully designed, with enough space around the bed and desk. The positives outweighed the drawbacks, but the hotel is not perfect.

reviews.title: Stylish with caveats
reviews.text: I stayed at The Emily Hotel during a local staycation. Value was the main drawback, especially for a short stay. The bed was comfortable, and the room layout worked well for unpacking and relaxing. Housekeeping did a strong job; towels, counters and floors were consistently clean. The positives outweighed the drawbacks, but the hotel is not perfect.

reviews.title: Worked well for this trip
reviews.text: Our trip brought us to The Emily Hotel for two nights. The Standard King Room, 290 sq ft matched the listing and made a clear first impression. We slept well, and the linens made the room feel more upscale. The breakfast setup felt underwhelming compared with the rest of the hotel. The positives outweighed the drawbacks, but the hotel is not perfect.

reviews.title: Not my best Chicago stay
reviews.text: Our trip brought us to The Emily Hotel for two nights. The hotel details, including rooftop lounge, terrace, fitness center and two restaurants, gave the stay a polished feel. Breakfast was available, but it did not feel worth planning around. I liked the hotel, but I would compare prices carefully before booking again. For the price, I expected fewer hassles.

reviews.title: Strong city stay
reviews.text: I stayed at The Emily Hotel during a trip with elderly parents. Being near Morgan station made the first day easier. The bathroom and bedding felt crisp, which made the stay easier to trust. The location is fun, but the room was not as quiet as I hoped. The location made it easy to walk to restaurants, transit and major sights.
